name: TestValidateSystemCall
title: TestValidateSystemCall
syntax: long TestValidateSystemCall (char workingDir[], char commandLine[], dword timeout);
description: This function can be used to start an external application. With the second function additionally you get the exit code of the external application. Once the application has been started successfully the system waits for it to exit at the end of the specified wait time. Test result of the command is reported.

You should either use the absolute path to the external application or use the call with the declaration of the working directory. To keep the configuration independent of installation paths on a concrete PC you can use <workingdir> alternatively to reference an environment variable of the target system, e.g. %MYAPP_DIR%.
The result of the command is reported.

parameters.name: workingDir
parameters.description: The working directory for the external application. The directory name may include spaces.

return: 1: The application exited with the expected exit code.

description: This test function is intended to invoke application calls in the test sequence. The provided command will be looked-up and executed on that platform, the user can access:
Standalone mode: Command is looked-up and executed on the standalone device.

Windows supplies exitcodes for applications in the range from 0 to 255. 255 is always supplied for applications which generate internal values outside this range (e.g., negative values). You might need to take this into account when setting the aExitcode parameter.
